entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop Pennisetum glaucum studied for blast resistance.

entities.name: BLAST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to resist infection by the fungal pathogen Magnaporthe grisea.

entities.name: MAGNAPORTHE GRISEA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen causing blast disease in pearl millet, specifically isolates Pg 45 and Pg 53.

entities.name: CROSSING
entities.description: The process of mating resistant and susceptible genotypes to generate F1, F2, and backcross generations for inheritance study.

entities.name: HALF DIALLEL
entities.description: A specific crossing scheme used to test for allelism among resistant genotypes.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating the reaction of plant generations against pathogen isolates in a controlled environment.

entities.name: INHERITANCE
entities.description: The study of how the blast resistance trait is transmitted from parents to offspring.

entities.name: ALLELISM
entities.description: The relationship where different versions of the same gene are identified across genotypes.

entities.name: SINGLE DOMINANT GENE
entities.description: The genetic unit identified as governing and conferring blast resistance in the studied genotypes.

relationships.description: is the cause of the disease resisted by
